executiveSummary:
KCE_Demo_Rebuild is a single-page item-management application split across a React frontend and a service-oriented backend (gateway, item service, audit service, PostgreSQL with trigger-based audit capture). The frontend sends an X-Persona header (Admin, User, Viewer) on every request; the gateway enforces role-based access on writes. Admin gets full create/update/delete; User can create and toggle completion but not delete; Viewer is read-only. All write operations are recorded automatically in an immutable audit trail that every persona can read.

The functional graph captures both halves: the human-facing personas (Admin, User, Viewer) driving the UI, and the System persona representing the backend handlers (REST routes, repositories, DB triggers). Each UI action is linked to the REST call it issues, giving end-to-end traceability from a button click to the gateway endpoint and the audit row it produces.
keyBusinessObjectives: Enforce role-based access at the gateway so write permissions follow the active persona, not client trust
Guarantee an immutable, automatically captured audit trail for every data mutation via database triggers
Keep the read path open to all personas so items and audit activity stay observable
Maintain a single dashboard surface that adapts its controls to the caller's role
Preserve end-to-end traceability from UI action to REST endpoint to audit record

keyStakeholders.interest: Full lifecycle control over items (create, update status, delete) plus visibility into the complete audit trail
Day-to-day item creation and completion tracking without destructive delete rights
Read-only oversight of item state and audit activity for monitoring and review
Correct handler behavior: input validation, existence checks, persistence, and trigger-based audit capture on every mutation

keyCapabilities: Persona-scoped RBAC enforced at the gateway via X-Persona header
Item CRUD with client-side validation and server-side existence checks
Completion-status toggling with immediate list refresh
Automatic audit capture through PostgreSQL AFTER-INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers
Open audit-log read access across all personas
Gateway health/status surfacing on the dashboard
Live persona switching that re-scopes data under the new role

Admin.description: Full-access role. Sees and uses every control on the dashboard: add form, completion toggle, and the Admin-only delete button, plus the audit log.

User.description: Standard role. Can create items and toggle completion status, but the delete control is hidden; retains full read access to items and audit logs.

Viewer.description: Read-only role. The add form and all row-level write controls are hidden; can browse items, watch gateway status, and read the audit trail.

System.description: Mechanical backend persona representing the gateway, item service, audit service, and database handlers that validate, persist, and audit each request.

06dd9b83-3e48-46e8-9aa1-df7098e3acb1.businessValue: Gives the standard User the full non-destructive item workflow: create, browse, toggle completion, and read the audit log. This is the primary day-to-day surface and the bulk of UI interaction.
06dd9b83-3e48-46e8-9aa1-df7098e3acb1.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  U[User] -->|create / toggle| F[Item Dashboard]
  F -->|POST/PUT /api/items| G[Gateway]
  F -->|GET /api/items, /api/audit| G
  G --> I[Item Service]
  G --> A[Audit Service]
  I --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]
  DB -->|trigger| AL[(audit_logs)]

ce3c7fe7-d223-4722-817f-0848f36e4b9a.businessValue: Backend item CRUD with existence validation and trigger-driven audit capture. Ensures every create, update, and delete is persisted correctly and recorded, independent of which UI persona initiated it.
ce3c7fe7-d223-4722-817f-0848f36e4b9a.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  R[REST Route] --> S[Item Service]
  S --> Repo[Item Repository]
  Repo --> DB[(items table)]
  DB -->|AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE| T[audit_trigger_func]
  T --> AL[(audit_logs)]

70432abd-1ad4-4196-80d3-6aafc0c62550.businessValue: Exposes the recorded audit trail through the audit service. Turns the trigger-captured rows into a readable, chronological feed for oversight.
70432abd-1ad4-4196-80d3-6aafc0c62550.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  Req[GET /api/audit] --> AS[Audit Service]
  AS --> ARepo[Audit Repository]
  ARepo --> AL[(audit_logs)]
  ARepo -->|ordered desc, limit| Resp[AuditLogResponse list]

e6449cb3-57d5-4e9b-8b3d-90a44a25b527.businessValue: Lightweight liveness probe confirming the backend process is up. Drives the dashboard gateway-status indicator.
e6449cb3-57d5-4e9b-8b3d-90a44a25b527.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  Probe[GET /] --> H[Health Handler]
  H -->|200 status ok| Resp[message: Backend API is running]

4b1451a7-b718-42d7-bb68-cfe6faee3fb1.businessValue: Admin's full item lifecycle: browse, create, toggle, and the exclusive delete capability. Delete is the one control gated to Admin only, making this the privileged management surface.
4b1451a7-b718-42d7-bb68-cfe6faee3fb1.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  Adm[Admin] -->|create / toggle / delete| F[Item Dashboard]
  F -->|X-Persona: Admin| G[Gateway]
  G -->|RBAC allow writes| I[Item Service]
  I --> DB[(items)]
  DB -->|trigger| AL[(audit_logs)]

19355ac1-4e1f-4780-9e7b-5defc7543014.businessValue: Admin review of the audit trail. Surfaces every recorded mutation with timestamp, action, table, and record id for accountability.
19355ac1-4e1f-4780-9e7b-5defc7543014.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  Adm[Admin] -->|view| AC[Audit Log Card]
  AC -->|GET /api/audit| G[Gateway]
  G --> AS[Audit Service]
  AS --> AL[(audit_logs)]

12e6c277-5f49-4e57-88e8-aa27d8cd855c.businessValue: Viewer's read-only oversight: browse items, confirm gateway connectivity, read the audit trail, and switch the active persona to observe RBAC behavior. No write controls are rendered.
12e6c277-5f49-4e57-88e8-aa27d8cd855c.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  V[Viewer] -->|browse / read| F[Item Dashboard]
  F -->|X-Persona: Viewer| G[Gateway]
  G -->|reads allowed| I[Item Service]
  G --> A[Audit Service]
  F -->|switch persona| F
